title: Fagara macrosperma
taxonomy: Fagara macrosperma belongs to the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification places Fagara macrosperma in the order Sapindales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the citrus and cashew families. The genus Fagara is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa, and Fagara macrosperma is a species within this group. Fagara macrosperma is also classified as a shrub or small tree, which is typical of many species within the Rutaceae family. By understanding the classification of Fagara macrosperma, botanists can better understand its evolutionary relationships and ecological role within its native habitat.

intro: Fagara macrosperma, also known as the Chinese prickly ash or prickly pepper, is a species of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Fagara macrosperma include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
